value_chain_position: Precision aerospace structures manufacturer — engine components (blades, vanes, discs), fasteners, and airframe structures for both commercial OEM and military platforms; sells engineered parts with long-term supply agreements.
relative_comps: Howmet occupies a critical chokepoint in aerospace: its nickel superalloy turbine blades and engine components are specified into programs (GE LEAP, F135) for decades, making switching nearly impossible mid-program. Revenue growing 14% YoY with 17% FCF margin — best FCF conversion in the aerostructures peer group. At $103B market cap it is richly valued vs. Triumph (TGI) and Spirit AeroSystems (SPR), which trade at distress multiples. The defense_tech theme fit is genuine — the F-35 engine component exposure and munitions-structure work tie to rearmament capex — though HWM is more of a critical supplier than a software-defined disruptor.
